title: GAO-09-149: Social Security Disability — Collection of Medical Evidence Could Be Improved (2008)
description: Captured gao.gov product page for GAO-09-149 (published/released Dec 17, 2008): Highlights and the two Recommendations for Executive Action. GAO evaluated DDS challenges in collecting medical records from claimants' own providers, in obtaining high-quality consultative exams, and SSA's progress moving from paper to electronic medical-evidence collection. Method: surveyed 51 DDS directors, visited 5 state DDSs, reviewed sample case files, interviewed SSA/DDS/association officials. Both recommendations (identify and evaluate promising DDS evidence-collection practices; evaluate low uptake of online submission) are recorded as Closed – Implemented. Directly relevant to 509 DDS medical-evidence workflow and processing timeliness.

source.title: Social Security Disability: Collection of Medical Evidence Could Be Improved with Evaluations to Identify Promising Collection Practices (GAO-09-149)

notes: Authoritative: this is a captured gao.gov product page for a published GAO report (GAO-09-149; published and publicly released Dec 17, 2008), containing GAO's own Highlights and Recommendations text. Single publication year so temporal_coverage start==end (2008). Capture retains gao.gov site chrome (nav/footer) around the report content. Base record of the gao-09-149 family; -full is the full PDF text and -summary is our condensation. Recommendation table is narrative, not a declared column schema.
